content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and an integer KKK.

In one operation, you may select an integer 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N and increase AiA_iAi​ by 111.

After performing all operations, create a bar graph-like shape as follows:


First, add a rectangle with width 111 and height A1A_1A1​ bounded by x=0,x=1,y=0,y=A1x=0, x=1, y=0, y=A_1x=0,x=1,y=0,y=A1​.
For each 2≤i≤N2 \leq i \leq N2≤i≤N, append a 1×Ai1\times A_i1×Ai​ rectangle to the right of the current shape, bounded by the lines x=i−1,x=i,y=0,y=Aix=i-1, x=i, y=0, y=A_ix=i−1,x=i,y=0,y=Ai​.
It can be shown that this shape has a contiguous area, and thus its perimeter is well defined.

Output the minimum number of operations such that the perimeter of this shape is **at most** KKK. Output −1-1−1 if this is impossible no matter what operations are done.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK, the size of the array AAA and the maximum allowed perimeter.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations, or −1-1−1 if no set of operations can make the condition hold.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Initially the perimeter is 121212, but after we update A2A_2A2​ by adding 111, the new perimeter is 101010. Thus, one operation is sufficient.

Test Case 2 : It can be proven to be impossible.
